title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT moment" is coming for robotics but warns against over-focusing on humanoid robots as the sole vision for the future. She advocates for designing robots prioritizing human interaction and delight over utility alone, suggesting that billions of robots will reshape how we approach robotic design and deployment.
writeup: Catie Cuan of Stanford contends that robotics is approaching a "ChatGPT moment," but argues the field's central challenge is not raw intelligence. She cautions against treating humanoid robots as the only template for the future, instead advocating designs that prioritize human interaction and delight rather than utility alone. The core claim: as robots scale to the billions, the priorities of robotic design and deployment will shift accordingly.
